title: What Used to Light Me Up That I Have Put Down?
subtitle: A journal practice for recovering the things that brought joy before they were set aside
excerpt: A journal practice for identifying the activities and interests that produced joy in the past but have been laid down, not because they stopped mattering, but because life became full of other things.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you are aware of a flatness in daily life but can't identify what's missing, or when you want to recover a sense of what you actually enjoy.

content.text: A journal practice for recovering the things that used to light you up. The activities and interests that produced genuine pleasure in the past often get set down gradually without a decision being made. Life fills. The thing moves from weekly to occasional to not at all, and then one day you realise it has been years. This practice goes looking for what is there to be picked back up.
List everything you used to do regularly that you no longer do at all, or rarely.
Any period of your past life. Hobbies, activities, ways of spending time. Write freely and don't filter for feasibility.
For each one on the list, write: 'I stopped because...'
Sometimes it's a real constraint. Sometimes it's inertia. Sometimes the reason you wrote down is not the real one. Notice the difference.
Which one, if you started again, would give you the most?
Not the most impressive, the most achievable, or the most justifiable. The one whose absence you feel most. Write what it is.
What is the smallest possible version of that thing you could do this week?
Not the full version. Twenty minutes. One attempt. The version that requires so little it is almost impossible to avoid. Write it down and commit to a time.
